subject: [PATCH v2 1/2] mm/vmstat, mm/memcontrol: add _monotonic vmstat readers

2026-07-13.patch_summary: This patch adds new functions to the Linux kernel that provide monotonic, non-clamping readers for certain vmstat counters. These counters are used by some applications to track monotonically-incremented event counts and compute deltas between two samples. Without these new functions, a signed-long wraparound could cause incorrect results when computing these deltas. The patch adds three new functions: global_node_page_state_monotonic(), node_page_state_monotonic(), and lruvec_page_state_monotonic(). These functions are only safe for use with monotonically-incremented counters and do not clamp negative reads to zero, unlike the existing clamped helpers. This patch is a prerequisite for further changes that will replace producer-side accumulators with read-side accumulators in prepare_scan_control().

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner
reviews.summary: Reviewer Johannes Weiner noted that the current implementation mixes state counts and monotonic events in node_stat_item, which can't be easily made per-node due to 'global' events; he considered this patch a good solution for now.
reviews.raw_body:
Acked-by: Johannes Weiner <hannes@cmpxchg.org>

It's unfortunate that we mix state counts with monotonic events in
node_stat_item. We have vm_event_state for monotonics, but they aren't
tracked per-node (which we need here and for other places in vmscan),
and they can't easily be made so because there are "global" events in
there that don't easily map to a specific node.

So this seems like the best solution for now.

2026-07-15.patch_summary: This patch adds new functions to the Linux kernel that allow reading monotonic, incrementing event counters without clamping negative values. This is necessary because clamped reads can lead to incorrect results when computing deltas across signed-long wraparounds. The new functions are only safe for monotonically-incremented counters and do not change the behavior of non-monotonic page-count readers. The patch also adds a prerequisite for replacing producer-side accumulators with read-side accumulators in the following patch.
